name: LINE1 cDNA drove IFN signaling and lamivudine reduced aged-mouse inflammation
summary: LINE1 derepression links senescence to innate immune activation, and lamivudine reduced age-associated inflammatory activation in old mice.
statement: In senescent cells, LINE1 derepression generated cytoplasmic cDNA that activated type I interferon signaling; in aged mice, lamivudine downregulated type I interferon activation and age-associated inflammation across several tissues.
population_or_model: Senescent human cells and aged mice
time_horizon: Late senescence and aged-mouse treatment experiments
quantitative_note: The published abstract reports tissue-level downregulation of IFN-I activation and inflammaging after lamivudine but does not make a human functional claim.
caveats: The organismal intervention evidence is mouse biomarker evidence.
Lamivudine has antiviral pharmacology and possible off-target aging interpretation boundaries.
The result does not establish durable function, disease prevention, or lifespan benefit.
